MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar Jan-20-2022 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. BRIAN R. PAGET, Esq. of Paladin Law Group LLP (Tel: 805-898-9700) for plaintiffs;
Atty. JODI E. LAMBERT, Esq. of Hirsch Closson, APLC (Tel: 619-233-7006) for defendant Insurance Company of the West;
Atty. LAUREN M. CASE, Esq. of Duane Morris LLP (Tel: 415-957-3000) for defendant/cross-complainant Alliuanze Global Risks US Insurance Company, f/k/a Allianz Insurance Company;
Atty. BLAISE S. CURET, Esq. of Sinnott, Puebla, Campagne & Curet, APLC (Tel: 415-352-6200) for Zurich American Insurance Company.

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFFS MILLER MARITAL DEDUCTION TRUST AND HELEN MILLER'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiffs' motion for summary adjudication is denied. Plaintiffs fail to maintain their burden of production.

Plaintiffs' notice of motion seeks summary adjudication of the following issues:

"1. Zurich, Allianz, and ICW had a duty to defend Plaintiffs against the DuBois Counterclaim in the Underlying Action.

2. Zurich, Allianz, and ICW's duty to defend Plaintiffs against the DuBois Counterclaim in the Underlying Action encompassed a duty to pay for independent, or Cumis, counsel."

Plaintiffs fail to cite competent evidence that they tendered the DuBois Counterclaim to ICW. At undisputed facts 34-35, plaintiffs rely on exhibit 19 to the Stone declaration to aver that ICW had constructive notice of the counterclaim. Exhibit 19 is a letter from ICW's counsel stating in pertinent part, "yesterday's email was the first notice ICW received of a 'tender' by your clients, Helen Miller and the Miller Marital Deduction Trust, of the RWQCB claim." (emphasis added). The letter makes no reference to the counterclaim. Plaintiffs therefore fail to maintain their burden as to the above issues, which are stated in the conjunctive. The Court rejects plaintiffs' attempt to introduce new evidence with its reply. (See San Diego Watercrafts, Inc. v. Wells Fargo Bank, N.A. (2002) 102 Cal.App.4th 308, 316.)

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, Email: maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/EPS)

RULING - A R G U E D; DEFENDANT ZURICH AMERICAN INSURANCE COMPANY'S ANTI SLAPP MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT IS TAKEN UNDER SUBMISSION. Judge: Richard Ulmer; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR # 8600 =302/ru

* * * After hearing the court issued an order that tracts the tentative ruling as follows: The insurers' anti-SLAPP motion to strike is DENIED. The insurers meet prong 1 of the anti-SLAPP statute. Plaintiffs' complaint includes numerous references to the insurers' communications with counsel during the federal action and asserts them as a basis for liability. (Complaint, pars. 9, 103-112). Plaintiffs' complaint does more than make collateral or incidental allusions to protected activity and a bad faith action can be subject to the anti-SLAPP statute where the basis for liability is judicial communications. (See Seltzer v. Barnes (2010) 182 Cal.App.4th 953, 968.) Plaintiff demonstrates a probability of prevailing on the merits and therefore, meets prong 2 of the anti-SLAPP statute. Paragraph 6 of the Stone declaration states that the insurers have not paid all required costs. In addition, that declaration and attachments show a conflict of interest that warrants the appointment of Cumis counsel. The insurers fail to show that the litigation privilege bars the entirety of plaintiffs' claims. * * *
